text.tspan: STATIC CONTENT HOSTING: don't serve static assets from your app servers.
Serve them DIRECTLY from blob storage (S3/Azure Blob/GCS) + CDN.
BAD: app serves all assets
CLIENT u2192 APP SERVER u2192 disk reads images/CSS/JS
u2192 every static asset request uses an app server thread
u2192 the same 100 KB CSS file fetched 1M times from your app
u2192 wasted bandwidth, memory, CPU

g.text: GOOD: serve static assets from blob + CDN

text.tspan: YOUR APP SERVER
serves only DYNAMIC content (API responses, server-rendered HTML)
static asset URLs reference CDN
free of static-content load
WHY THIS WINS
u2022 app servers handle ONLY dynamic traffic u2192 smaller, fewer
u2022 static assets cached at edge u2192 fast global delivery
u2022 cost: object storage + CDN is cheaper than app-server bandwidth
u2022 deployments via blob upload, not app redeploy
u2022 cache busting via content-hash URLs: immutable forever-cacheable assets
PATTERN VARIANTS
u2022 Single-Page App: HTML + JS shell served from CDN; API calls to backend
u2022 Server-rendered + static: SSR HTML from app; assets from CDN
u2022 Static-site generator: prebuilt HTML to CDN (Hugo, Jekyll, Astro, 11ty)
u2022 Jamstack: build-time static + dynamic via APIs
u2022 Edge functions: compute at CDN edge (Workers, Lambda@Edge)
DEPLOY PIPELINE
build step generates static assets
pipeline UPLOADS to blob store (S3 / Azure Blob / GCS)
with content-hash filename (cache-bust forever)
e.g. /assets/main.a3f8c9.js
BLOB STORE (S3 / Azure Blob / GCS / R2)
cheap object storage; 99.99% available;
scales infinitely; pay per GB stored + GB transferred
CDN (CloudFront, Cloudflare, Fastly, Akamai, Azure CDN)
caches blob content at edge PoPs worldwide
origin = blob store; CDN handles most traffic
see [CDN page](../scale/cdn.md)
CLIENT
HTML from app: <script src='https://cdn.example.com/main.a3f8c9.js'>
Browser fetches script from CDN (u226550ms global)
CDN cache hit: served from edge
CDN miss: CDN fetches from blob; re-caches
